XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX coins XX/XX/XXXX I purchased XXXX ; XXXX ounce XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX coins from a website; XXXX for {$1700.00} using my Barclay Bank World Elite Mastercard. I noticed the label from XXXX that raised a red flag. I immediately tested the coins with a magnet ( REFRIGERATOR MAGNET ) they failed the magnet test, red flag number XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX are XXXX % pure silver. Silver is a non-ferrous metal which has no iron content and is not able to be attracted by a magnet.

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX.

I did as instructed. They said they will contact returns department XXXX email me a return shipping label ( never happened ). The actual verbiage ; It is important XXXX  note that simple tests such as magnet checks or the use of basic shop tools can sometimes produce misleading results. XX/XX/XXXX, I went back XXXX  the scammers website chat function and tried for XXXX minutes to contact an agent with no response. I have screenshots on my phone if needed. 
They basically revealed their fraud because no XXXX  % pure silver coins can be attracted by a magnet. Early XXXX XXXX Bank XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX refuted my dispute in favor of the scammers. I then requested a XXXX several times due to call back appointment hit and miss. The XXXX rejected my claim.

Something I wrote in my letter to the Barclay Bank World Elite Mastercard XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ; Early XX/XX/XXXX silver prices peaked at {$110.00} an ounce.
